text: 34g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
6.2g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for a dairy food, this is doing the right stuff.
6.2g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
vitamin b12 at 10% daily value, vitamin a at 22% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
calcium at 10% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

the upsides first: easy protein delivery for normal diets. dairy gets real credit for convenience. can be a very convenient protein vehicle. convenience is one of dairy’s strongest practical advantages. clear calcium identity. that is a core category strength.

on the weaker side: fat quality varies wildly across dairy. some dairy is clean, some really is not. some versions get dense fast on fat, sodium, or both. the category swings a lot by format. processing changes the category a lot. the label alone does not tell the whole story.
C tier. Best only when the taste or format matters more than getting the strongest dairy profile.
